# @zakkster/lite-headless/pagination

Headless pagination control: page-N-of-M navigation with prev/next/
first/last buttons + a dynamic page-number list with automatic
ellipsis insertion for long ranges. The primitive owns the
navigation logic and ARIA wiring; the consumer renders the page
list (or uses the wrapper which renders for you).

## Quick example

```js
import { createPagination } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/pagination";

const pg = createPagination({
    pageCount:    20,
    defaultPage:   1,
    siblingCount:  1,    // pages each side of current
    boundaryCount: 1,    // pages at start/end
    onChange: (page, reason) => loadResults(page),
});

pg.attachRoot    (navEl);     // role=navigation, aria-label
pg.attachPrev    (prevBtn);   // data-disabled at page 1
pg.attachNext    (nextBtn);   // data-disabled at last page
pg.attachFirst   (firstBtn);  // jumps to 1
pg.attachLast    (lastBtn);   // jumps to total
pg.attachPageList(listEl);    // consumer renders <li>s into this

// Render the page items list (consumer)
function render() {
    listEl.innerHTML = "";
    for (const item of pg.items()) {
        const li = document.createElement("li");
        if (item.type === "ellipsis") {
            li.textContent = "…";
        } else {
            const btn = document.createElement("button");
            btn.textContent = String(item.page);
            li.appendChild(btn);
            pg.markPage(btn, item.page);     // wire click + aria-current
        }
        listEl.appendChild(li);
    }
}
```

The `<lite-pagination>` wrapper does the render automatically.

## Options

| Option           | Default | Notes                                                |
| ---------------- | ------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |
| `pageCount`      | `1`     | Total pages (positive integer)                       |
| `defaultPage`    | `1`     | Uncontrolled seed (clamped to range)                 |
| `page`           | -       | Signal<number> for controlled mode (read-only)       |
| `siblingCount`   | `1`     | Pages either side of current (non-negative)          |
| `boundaryCount`  | `1`     | Pages at start/end (non-negative)                    |
| `onChange`       | -       | `(page, reason) => void` on page change              |
| `onItemsChange`  | -       | `(items) => void` whenever the items array changes   |

`reason` values: `"set"`, `"next"`, `"prev"`, `"first"`, `"last"`,
`"click"` (from a page button), `"attribute"` (from the wrapper's
attribute observer).

## items() algorithm

`items()` returns an array describing what to render. Short-
circuit: if `total <= 2*sibling + 2*boundary + 3`, all pages are
shown without ellipsis. Otherwise the algorithm renders four
ranges with single-page-gap merging:

| Configuration                            | items()                            |
| ---------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------- |
| 5 pages, sibling=1, boundary=1, p=3      | `1 2 [3] 4 5`                      |
| 20 pages, sibling=1, boundary=1, p=1     | `[1] 2 … 20`                       |
| 20 pages, sibling=1, boundary=1, p=10    | `1 … 9 [10] 11 … 20`               |
| 20 pages, sibling=1, boundary=1, p=20    | `1 … 19 [20]`                      |
| 10 pages, sibling=1, boundary=1, p=4     | `1 2 3 [4] 5 … 10`                 |
| 20 pages, sibling=1, boundary=2, p=10    | `1 2 … 9 [10] 11 … 19 20`          |
| 10 pages, sibling=1, boundary=0, p=5     | `4 [5] 6`                          |
| 50 pages, sibling=3, boundary=2, p=25    | `1 2 … 22 23 24 [25] 26 27 28 … 49 50` |

Item shape:

```ts
type Item =
    | { type: "page";     page: number; current: boolean }
    | { type: "ellipsis"; position: "left" | "right" }
```

Single-page-gap merging: when the gap between a boundary and the
sibling range is exactly one page, the algorithm renders the
intermediate page directly instead of an ellipsis (because an
ellipsis hiding a single page wastes a slot that could just be the
page).

## Attachment methods

| Method                 | Effect                                                               |
| ---------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `attachRoot(el)`       | role=navigation, aria-label="Pagination" (unless overridden)         |
| `attachPrev(el)`       | data-disabled at page 1; click → prev()                              |
| `attachNext(el)`       | data-disabled at last page; click → next()                           |
| `attachFirst(el)`      | data-disabled at page 1; click → first()                             |
| `attachLast(el)`       | data-disabled at last page; click → last()                           |
| `attachPageList(el)`   | role=list; container for the dynamically-rendered page buttons      |
| `markPage(el, page)`   | wires click + aria-current paint; returns OFF cleanup function       |

`markPage` returns a cleanup function — the wrapper calls it to
detach the previous page buttons before re-rendering. If you
manage the render yourself, save the cleanup from each `markPage`
and call them all before rebuilding the list.

## ARIA

| Element     | Attributes                                                  |
| ----------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| Root        | `role="navigation"`, `aria-label="Pagination"`              |
| Nav buttons | `aria-label="Go to {previous/next/first/last} page"`, `aria-disabled`, real `disabled` (when target is a `<button>`) |
| Page list   | `role="list"`                                               |
| Page button | `aria-current="page"` when current; `aria-label="Go to page N"` (unless overridden); `data-current="true"` for CSS |
| Ellipsis    | `<span aria-hidden="true">…</span>` (wrapper-rendered)      |

## Imperative API

```js
pg.setPage(n, reason?)
pg.setPageCount(n)        // clamps current page if it overflows
pg.first() / pg.last()
pg.next() / pg.prev()

pg.page()                 // reactive accessor
pg.pageCount()
pg.items()                // current Item[]

pg.destroy()
```

## Custom element wrapper

```html
<lite-pagination page-count="50" page="1" sibling-count="1" boundary-count="1">
    <button data-pgn-first>«</button>
    <button data-pgn-prev>‹</button>
    <ol data-pgn-list></ol>
    <button data-pgn-next>›</button>
    <button data-pgn-last>»</button>
</lite-pagination>
```

The wrapper renders `<li>` items into `[data-pgn-list]` automatically
on every page or pageCount change. Each rendered `<li>` contains
either a `<button data-pgn-page="N">` or a `<span aria-hidden>…</span>`.

### Attributes

| Attribute        | Effect                                                  |
| ---------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
| `page-count`     | Reactive: set to change total pages                     |
| `page`           | Reactive: set to navigate to a page                     |
| `sibling-count`  | Pages either side of current (initial only)             |
| `boundary-count` | Pages at start/end (initial only)                       |

### Imperative API on host

```js
host.setPage(n, reason?)
host.setPageCount(n)
host.first() / host.last() / host.next() / host.prev()
host.page         // read-only
host.pageCount    // read-only
host.items        // read-only Item[]
```

### Dispatched events

- `change` — `{ detail: { page, reason } }`
- `itemschange` — `{ detail: { items } }`

## CSS contract

```css
/* Nav buttons */
[data-pgn-prev], [data-pgn-next],
[data-pgn-first], [data-pgn-last] { /* base styles */ }
[data-pgn-prev][data-disabled],
[data-pgn-next][data-disabled],
[data-pgn-first][data-disabled],
[data-pgn-last][data-disabled] {
    opacity: 0.4; cursor: not-allowed;
}

/* Page buttons */
[data-pgn-page] { /* base */ }
[data-pgn-page][data-current="true"] {
    /* active style */
}

/* Ellipsis */
[data-pgn-ellipsis] { /* spacer style */ }
```

## When NOT to use

- **Infinite scroll / load-more**: pagination is for discrete page
  navigation. For infinite scroll, use intersection observer
  patterns directly.
- **Tab-style page switcher** (a fixed small N, e.g., 3-5 pages):
  use the `tabs` primitive — it has roving tabindex and the right
  ARIA pattern for that case.
- **Form pagination wizards** (step 1 of 5): use `stepper` instead;
  it has step validation and is semantically closer to "wizard"
  than "pagination".
